answer:
When \(R^2 = 0\), the regression model explains none of the variability in \(y\) around its mean, so (iii) is true. This implies all slope coefficients are zero, making the fitted line horizontal with respect to each explanatory variable, so (i) and (ii) are also true. The intercept is not forced to be zero; it simply equals the mean of \(y\), so (iv) is false.

\boxed{J}
